reasons: Karrie punishes Edith's tank/frontline nature because repeated attacks can trigger max-HP true damage.
Edith becomes weaker when she cannot safely absorb damage and transform before Karrie melts her HP.

proof.summary: Edith wants to stay durable long enough to start fights and transform, while Karrie's repeated attacks can convert into max-HP true damage that reduces Edith's tank value.
worksBestWhen: Karrie has peel from a roamer or frontline.
Karrie keeps distance from Edith's crowd-control range.
failureCases: Edith catches Karrie first with crowd control.
Karrie fights without protection before her attack-speed items are ready.

reasons: Diggie reduces Edith's teamfight engage value by protecting allies from her crowd-control chain.
Time Journey is especially valuable when Edith tries to start with crowd control before transforming.

proof.summary: Edith wants to create a crowd-control window for her team, while Diggie can remove debuffs and give nearby allies control immunity during the engage window.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves Time Journey for Edith's real engage.
Diggie positions near the allies Edith wants to catch.
failureCases: Edith baits Time Journey before committing.
Edith catches Diggie first or forces the fight while Diggie's ultimate is unavailable.

reasons: Valir can push Edith away before she reaches ideal crowd-control range.
His slows and knockback make it harder for Edith to force a clean Phylax engage.

proof.summary: Edith wants to enter crowd-control range, while Valir can slow, zone, and knock her away before she reaches the backline.
worksBestWhen: Valir keeps Searing Torrent for Edith's approach.
The team follows Valir's disengage instead of re-entering Edith's range.
failureCases: Edith flanks from fog or uses a surprise engage angle.
Valir is controlled or burst before he can disengage.

reasons: Dyrroth can pressure Edith before her teamfight value comes online.
His defense reduction helps punish Edith's tank form before she gets a comfortable transform window.

proof.summary: Edith relies on durability to enter fights, while Dyrroth can reduce her physical defense and burst her during short punish windows.
worksBestWhen: Dyrroth engages after Edith misses or spends a crowd-control skill.
failureCases: Dyrroth dives into Edith's crowd control without backup.
Edith survives the burst and turns the fight after transforming.

reasons: Lunox can punish Edith with burst windows while avoiding key retaliation with Brilliance.
Brilliance gives Lunox an answer when Edith tries to turn the fight after transforming.

proof.summary: Edith wants enemies to stay inside her retaliation window, while Lunox can use Brilliance to avoid key timing and Darkening to reposition for burst.
worksBestWhen: Lunox holds Brilliance for Edith's engage or burst window.
Lunox attacks after Edith has already committed her crowd control.
failureCases: Edith catches Lunox before she can enter her safe ultimate state.
